articles.title: SL Green to Sell 10 East 53rd Street to Meadow Partners for $312M
articles.narrative: SL Green Realty is selling 10 East 53rd Street, a 37-story Midtown East office tower, to Meadow Partners for $312.2 million. The transaction, expected to close in Q3 2026, is part of SL Green's strategic disposition plan and will generate approximately $100 million in net cash proceeds for corporate debt repayment. SL Green will continue to manage the 390,000-square-foot Class A asset, which is currently 92% leased.

data_points.address: 10 East 53rd Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Class A office asset located in Midtown East between Fifth and Madison avenues.
data_points.project_notes: SL Green acquired the property in 2012 for $252.5 million and later took over its partner's stake in 2014. A $170 million redevelopment was undertaken to reposition the building.

articles.title: Walker & Dunlop Leads $16.5B Fannie Mae Lending Market
articles.narrative: Walker & Dunlop has emerged as the leading Fannie Mae multifamily lender for 2026 year-to-date, originating $2.18 billion across 110 loans, according to CRED iQ's proprietary loan analytics. The firm's performance highlights a concentrated agency lending market where the top 10 originators captured approximately 78% of the total $16.5 billion volume through mid-May. The market is heavily driven by refinancing activity, which accounts for 62.8% of the volume, as borrowers address upcoming maturities and replace bridge debt. Gateway and Sun Belt metros, including New York, San Jose, Los Angeles, Phoenix, Miami, and Dallas, are the primary destinations for this agency capital.

articles.title: Patrick Southern of Serhant: 5 Questions
articles.narrative: This article features an interview with Patrick Southern, a veteran residential broker who recently joined Serhant to expand his new development sales business in Jersey City, NJ. Southern discusses his 20-year career in the market, highlighting its evolution from an affordable option to a thriving urban center. He emphasizes his high-volume transaction approach, having closed 376 contracts in 2025, and explains how Serhant's platform provides the necessary support to pursue larger-scale projects that were previously out of reach.

articles.title: Behind Blackstone’s Newfound Commitment to Finance Home Construction
articles.narrative: Blackstone's Real Estate Debt Strategies (BREDS) platform is launching a new initiative to finance the construction of over 50,000 for-sale single-family homes annually across the U.S. This move aims to address the nation's critical housing supply shortage, which has seen new supply fall significantly and fewer homes built today than in 1960, despite population growth. The firm, through its portfolio company Brio Homebuilder Solutions and various third-party partners, seeks to be part of the solution to the housing crisis, especially as regional banks have pulled back from construction financing. This initiative also comes amidst political scrutiny and public blame directed at institutional investors for contributing to housing affordability issues.

articles.title: S3 Capital Lends $132M on Washington Heights Student Housing Development
articles.narrative: Edge Property Group secured $131.5 million in construction financing from S3 Capital for a 28-story student housing development in Washington Heights, New York City. The loan will fund the construction of 276 units and 321 beds, and also refinance existing debt on the project. S3 Capital noted the severe supply-demand imbalance in the Washington Heights student housing market, which has a very low vacancy rate and limited new deliveries.

data_points.address: 465 West 165th Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: The building will include over 11,000 square feet of amenity space, such as private study pods, quiet lounges, a fitness center, a golf simulator, and an outdoor terrace.
data_points.project_notes: The loan also refinances past debt on the property.

articles.market_intelligence: Washington Heights is one of the most supply-constrained neighborhoods in New York City for residential housing, with a vacancy rate of 0.8 percent as of Q2 2025. Only 14 units were delivered in the prior 12 months.

articles.title: JLL Negotiates Sale of 838,446 SF Industrial Facility in Katy, Texas
articles.narrative: JLL has facilitated the sale of an 838,446-square-foot industrial facility in Katy, Texas. The property, developed as a build-to-suit for a building materials supplier, was acquired by Goldman Sachs from a partnership between Pinpoint Commercial and Senterra. This transaction represents the first phase of the larger Grand Central West development.

data_points.notable_features: Features 40-foot clear heights, 136 dock doors, 20 loading ramps, three drive-in doors, 185-foot truck court depths, and 31,065 square feet of office space.
data_points.project_notes: Constructed as a build-to-suit for Builders FirstSource. This facility is Phase I of the Grand Central West development.

articles.title: Partnership Purchases 1.3 MSF Industrial Property in Phoenix
articles.narrative: A joint venture formed by Machine Investment Group, Miramar Capital, and Axonic Capital has acquired The Hub @ 202, a 1.3-million-square-foot industrial park in Phoenix. The new ownership plans to invest additional capital to create move-in ready industrial suites to meet market demand and attract a diverse tenant base. The transaction terms were not disclosed.

data_points.notable_features: Industrial park features 10 buildings ranging from 65,000 SF to 270,000 SF.

articles.title: PCCP, Alliance Residential Buy 184-Unit Multifamily Community in Riverside, California
articles.narrative: A joint venture between PCCP Multifamily and Alliance Residential Co. has acquired The Hawthorne, a 184-unit multifamily community in Riverside, California, for $65.3 million. Completed in 2023, the property offers a mix of one-, two-, and three-bedroom floor plans with modern finishes and a comprehensive amenity package.

data_points.notable_features: Units feature quartz countertops, kitchen islands, stainless steel appliances with gas ranges, in-unit washers/dryers, vinyl plank flooring, and private patios or balconies. Community amenities include a pool and spa with cabanas, landscaped courtyards with barbecues and fire pits, a clubhouse, and a fitness center.

articles.title: Pebblebrook Sells Chamberlain West Hollywood Hotel Near Los Angeles for $43.5M
articles.narrative: Pebblebrook Hotel Trust has sold the Chamberlain West Hollywood Hotel in West Hollywood for $43.5 million to an undisclosed buyer. The transaction involves a 115-key hotel property.

articles.title: SimonCRE Acquires 10 Acres to Develop Prasada East Retail Project in Surprise, Arizona
articles.narrative: SimonCRE has acquired a 10-acre parcel in Surprise, Arizona, to develop Prasada East, a 98,000-square-foot open-air retail center. The project, which will break ground in spring 2026, will be anchored by a Whole Foods Market and include several other confirmed tenants, complementing the larger Prasada master-planned development.

data_points.notable_features: Open-air retail center anchored by Whole Foods Market, complementing the existing Village at Prasada and Prasada North developments.
data_points.project_notes: Initial phase will feature a 35,100-square-foot Whole Foods Market.

articles.title: Hanley Investment Group closes $5.3 million sale of retail center in Terre Haute
articles.narrative: Hanley Investment Group Real Estate Advisors facilitated the off-market sale of Eastview Commons, a newly constructed, fully occupied retail center in Terre Haute, Indiana, for $5.3 million. The property, anchored by a high-volume Chipotle, features a diverse tenant mix and benefits from its strategic location adjacent to a major retail anchor, drawing significant traffic to the area. The transaction highlights the continued investment interest in well-located, internet-resistant retail assets in growing markets.

data_points.notable_features: Newly constructed, fully occupied retail center anchored by a high-volume Chipotle with a drive-thru, positioned as a pad to a Walmart Supercenter.

companies_people.label: SELLER BROKER
companies_people.firm_name: Hanley Investment Group Real Estate Advisors

articles.title: Collies adds managing director of real estate services in Kansas City office
articles.narrative: Colliers has appointed Jeannette Vahle as managing director of Real Estate Management Services in its Kansas City office. Vahle brings over 30 years of experience in commercial real estate, including property management, asset services, and operations leadership. In her new role, she will be responsible for overseeing the firm's REMS platform, focusing on property operations, client relations, team leadership, and strategic growth initiatives within the Kansas City market.

people.name: Jeannette Vahle
people.title: Managing Director, Real Estate Management Services

articles.title: St. Louis’ S.M. Wilson & Co. earns 2026 Quality Concrete Award
articles.narrative: S. M. Wilson & Co. received a 2026 Quality Concrete Award from the Concrete Council of St. Louis & American Concrete Institute (ACI) Missouri Chapter for its work as Construction Manager at Risk on the Northwest R-1 School District's Proposition S Bond Program. The program involved updates across all district campuses, notably including multi-purpose storm shelter additions at several schools constructed with precast concrete panels. These panels were recognized for their strength, sustainability, and durability, allowing the spaces to double as severe weather shelters.

data_points.notable_features: Multi-purpose storm shelter additions constructed with precast concrete panels, providing strength, sustainability, and durability while also serving as safe places during severe weather.
data_points.project_notes: The Proposition S Bond Program included updates to every campus in the Northwest R-1 School District. The program was also a finalist for a St. Louis Council of Construction Consumers (SLC3) Show Me Award in the Outstanding Project Under $20 million category.

articles.title: Ryan Companies, Washington Capital Management start construction of 190,000-square-foot spec industrial facility in Bellwood
articles.narrative: Ryan Companies US, Inc. and Washington Capital Management, Inc. have commenced construction on a 190,000-square-foot speculative industrial facility in Bellwood, Illinois. This project, located at the former SureBuilt building site, continues Ryan's strategy of redeveloping underutilized industrial land. The facility is designed to attract a variety of industrial users, offering Class A amenities and strategic access to major interstates and O'Hare International Airport, addressing the demand for modern industrial space in a supply-constrained submarket.

data_points.notable_features: Class A amenities including 32-foot clear height, ESFR fire protection, and exterior bike racks, with flexible design for warehousing, distribution, and light manufacturing. Immediate access to Interstates 290, 294, and 88, and proximity to O'Hare International Airport.
data_points.project_notes: Located on the site of the former SureBuilt building.

articles.title: Seniors housing community opens in St. Francis
articles.narrative: A new senior living community, Vista Prairie at Eagle Pointe, has officially opened in St. Francis, Minnesota. The community offers independent living, assisted living, memory care, and care suites, providing a range of housing and care options for older adults. Located at 23440 Ambassador Boulevard, the development aims to address the increasing demand for senior housing in the area with a focus on resident wellness, comfort, and social connection.

data_points.notable_features: Offers independent living, assisted living, memory care, and care suites with common spaces and amenities focused on wellness, comfort, and social connection.

articles.title: June 2026
articles.narrative: This article is a placeholder and does not contain any real estate news or information. It appears to be a link to an external publication without any content to analyze.

articles.title: Developers: doing it for the fee
articles.narrative: This article examines the controversial practice of developers and sponsors collecting substantial fees even when their real estate investments result in significant losses for investors. It highlights how the "2 and 20" compensation model, common in finance, is applied in commercial real estate with additional construction and property management fees. Critics argue this structure can incentivize sponsors to prioritize asset accumulation for fee generation over investor profits, while proponents contend that fees are for services rendered, regardless of investment outcomes. The piece cites the Transamerica Pyramid sale, where the developer received an $80 million fee package despite the German pension funds losing over $200 million, and the case of a multifamily syndicator accused of overcharging fees amidst investor losses, as examples of this contentious dynamic.

companies_people.label: OTHER
companies_people.firm_name: New York University's Schack Institute of Real Estate

articles.title: Will AI kill commercial real estate?
articles.narrative: This article presents a nuanced debate on the potential impact of artificial intelligence on commercial real estate, particularly the office sector. Sarah Mancuso of JLL argues that while AI will displace some workflows, it will ultimately create new categories of work and drive demand for specialized real estate, particularly from AI-native companies and data centers. She emphasizes that human judgment and relationships remain crucial in CRE, which AI cannot replicate. Conversely, Vikram Malhotra of Mizuho Americas suggests that AI poses a significant risk to office demand by potentially slowing "office-using" job growth from historical rates to a much lower pace, impacting office, retail, and residential sectors as companies prioritize efficiency.

people.name: Sarah Mancuso
people.title: President, West Region, United States Leasing Advisory

articles.market_intelligence: Construction spending on offices is down from a 2020 peak, while outlays on data centers have increased and are now level with general office spending, according to U.S. Census Bureau data analyzed by Paul Kedrosky.

articles.title: “You’re right!” So far, AI’s threat to resi real estate is making clients overconfident
articles.narrative: This article explores how artificial intelligence, particularly generative AI tools like ChatGPT, is impacting residential real estate agents by making clients overconfident and increasing agent workloads. While previous technological disruptions like online listings and iBuying failed to displace agents or significantly reduce commissions, AI presents a new challenge by empowering clients with often inaccurate or legally non-compliant information. Agents are now spending more time correcting AI-generated advice, which could lead to increased workloads, lower commissions, or even jeopardize deals, despite the technology still being in its early stages of adoption.

articles.title: Editor’s note: Almost human
articles.narrative: This editorial discusses the impact of artificial intelligence on the real estate industry, highlighting both its potential and the anxieties it creates. The author notes that while AI bots can perform various tasks, they still exhibit some limitations. The article explores how AI is influencing residential real estate brokers by empowering clients and agents, potentially increasing workloads and complicating deals. It also touches on the demands of AI startup founders in the office leasing market and features a debate on whether AI will ultimately harm the industry. The piece also previews other topics in the June issue, including the Hamptons market and developer practices.

articles.title: Rockrose reels in $400M loan at 666 Greenwich
articles.narrative: Rockrose Development secured a $404 million refinancing loan from PNC Bank for its luxury multifamily property, The Archive, located at 666 Greenwich Street in the West Village. Freddie Mac is slated to purchase the loan. The property, a historic Romanesque Revival-style building, features 479 residential units and 80,000 square feet of retail space.

data_points.address: 666 Greenwich Street, New York City, NY
data_points.notable_features: Romanesque Revival-style red brick building, full block property, internal atrium, in-house cinema, parking, common roof deck, city landmark since 1967.
data_points.project_notes: The property was originally owned by the federal government for customs operations, then turned over to New York City in 1982. A partnership of New York State Urban Development Corporation and Teitelbaum Group was designated to convert the building into apartments. Rockrose joined as a general partner in a 99-year lease in 1985. The operating group pays into a fund for historic preservation and has a full property tax exemption.

articles.title: Runyon Group’s plan for record Water Mill retail buy
articles.narrative: The Runyon Group, in a joint venture with River Oaks Properties, has acquired a 50,000-square-foot retail portfolio in Water Mill, NY, for $39 million. This acquisition, comprising 670 and 760 Montauk Highway, represents 84% of the leasable retail space in the hamlet and sets a new price record for the area. The buyers plan to revitalize the properties by restoring the buildings, curating a mix of both essential and high-end tenants, and enhancing the outdoor spaces to create a more inviting and community-focused shopping experience by summer 2027. The developers aim to balance luxury with daily needs, drawing on their experience in creating successful retail destinations in other affluent markets.

data_points.address: 670 and 760 Montauk Highway, Water Mill, NY
data_points.notable_features: The portfolio represents 84% of the leasable retail space in Water Mill and is strategically located on Montauk Highway, a key thoroughfare.
data_points.project_notes: The buyers plan to restore the buildings, introduce a mix of useful and chic tenants, and improve outdoor spaces, with completion targeted for summer 2027.

articles.title: Inside the wellness amenity business in the Hamptons
articles.narrative: The Hamptons real estate market has become a laboratory for integrating wellness amenities into luxury homes, driven by a cultural shift among wealthy homeowners seeking calm and restoration. New builds are allocating significant budget to features like infrared saunas, cold plunge pools, and meditation rooms, reflecting a growing demand from buyers across different age cohorts. Beyond specific amenities, architects and designers are focusing on creating peaceful, human-scaled spaces with natural materials, biophilic design, and advanced environmental systems like enhanced air filtration and circadian lighting, emphasizing that the overall architecture and flow contribute as much to wellness as individual features.

articles.title: Can the Hamptons keep its hot streak?
articles.narrative: The Hamptons luxury residential market experienced a strong start to 2026, building on momentum from late 2025, with transactions above $5 million achieving a record share of deals. The median sale price for luxury homes in the first quarter of 2026 jumped 30 percent to $13 million, and sales of $10 million or more generated $560 million in volume. Notable transactions included the $58 million sale of an oceanfront mansion in Bridgehampton to Boston Celtics co-owner Wyc Grousbeck and his wife, Emilia Fazzalari, and the $72 million sale of an East Hampton estate by Ann Tenenbaum, marking the most expensive closing of the year so far. Despite some broker skepticism about sustained pace due to macroeconomic factors, the market continues to see significant high-value deals, with the rental market also showing signs of recovery after two slower summers.
articles.market_intelligence: Transactions above $5 million scored a record share of deals in the Hamptons during the first three months of 2026. The Hamptons rental market is showing signs of regaining momentum after two sluggish summers, with some agents reporting increased lease activity for the upcoming summer season.

articles.title: Behind the Hamptons’ inventory mirage
articles.narrative: The Hamptons real estate market is experiencing a persistent inventory shortage, particularly for high-end properties, leading brokers to adopt creative strategies like land acquisition and custom home building. This scarcity is exacerbated by a strong 2025 sales year and the prevalence of off-market transactions for super-prime estates. While some brokers dismiss the inventory narrative as a symptom of increased competition among agents, others emphasize the critical role of broker networks in sourcing unlisted properties, which often represent the most significant deals in the region.

articles.title: Watch mogul’s ex-wife finds buyer for waterfront Gables Estates home
articles.narrative: A waterfront mansion in Gables Estates, previously owned by Martha Cecilia Hernandez Quintero and her ex-husband Rick De La Croix, has found a buyer. The nearly 12,000-square-foot luxury home was asking $28 million and was among the top contracts signed in Miami-Dade County last week. Rick De La Croix is a prominent watch marketer and entrepreneur.

data_points.notable_features: Waterfront mansion in Gables Estates with eight bedrooms and 10 bathrooms.

articles.title: “Parcel manipulation”: Homestead homeowners sue over developer’s two-decade control of HOA
articles.narrative: Homeowners at Villa Portofino East, a 117-unit townhouse complex in Homestead, FL, have filed a lawsuit alleging that the developer, Prime Homes at Villa Portofino East (an affiliate of Prime Group), has illegally maintained control of the HOA board for two decades. The suit claims the developer has manipulated parcel calculations by including land transferred for a new hotel development as part of the residential community's total parcels, thereby artificially delaying the statutory 90% conveyance threshold for homeowner turnover. Residents also allege neglect of common area maintenance under the developer-controlled board.

data_points.notable_features: Townhouse complex located near Florida's Turnpike.
data_points.project_notes: Developer is accused of manipulating parcel calculations to delay HOA turnover to homeowners. A 98-key Homewood Suites hotel is under construction on land previously part of the residential complex, expanding an existing 126-key Hampton Inn & Suites.

companies_people.label: DEVELOPER/SPONSOR
companies_people.firm_name: Prime Homes at Villa Portofino East

articles.title: South Florida’s top deals: Wellington estate trades for $17M
articles.narrative: This article is a roundup of top real estate transactions in South Florida. The top commercial deal was the sale of a 207,000-square-foot truck stop in Medley for $23.6 million. Additionally, another truck stop in Hialeah Gardens traded for $20 million, and a portfolio of industrial properties and vacant land in Fort Lauderdale sold for $11.3 million. The article also notes a trend in residential mortgage activity, with refinancing originations surpassing new purchase loans for the second consecutive quarter.

data_points.address: 12200 Northwest South River Drive, Medley, FL
data_points.notable_features: Comprises five parcels.
data_points.project_notes: Seller had owned the property since 2003.

articles.title: Is South Florida’s branded condo boom reaching its limit?
articles.narrative: This article explores whether South Florida's branded condo market is reaching its saturation point, using the Aston Martin Residences as a prime example of potential pitfalls. The author argues that while luxury branding can lend credibility, developers must deliver on promises, as unmet expectations can damage a project's reputation and resale value. The piece highlights ongoing legal disputes at Aston Martin Residences over alleged construction defects and undelivered amenities, as well as foreclosure challenges at the Mercedes-Benz Places project, suggesting that buyers and brokers are becoming more discerning about branded developments. The article concludes that successful branded projects require a clear brand identity, a strong developer track record, and a focus on delivering the promised lifestyle, with some experts suggesting that differentiation might now come from not having a brand in a saturated market.

articles.title: How Savas Er saved Mag Mile
articles.narrative: Savas Er, through his firm North American Real Estate Group, has strategically acquired several prominent retail properties in Chicago, including two major assets on the Magnificent Mile and a portfolio in Lakeview, following a period of significant distress in the city's retail sector. Er, who previously focused on turning around distressed suburban retail centers, capitalized on the high vacancy rates and foreclosures that impacted Chicago's prime retail corridors between 2020 and 2022. His methodical approach to distressed investments, often in partnership with Buzz Ruttenberg, positioned him to expand into the urban core, aiming to revitalize key retail landmarks.
articles.market_intelligence: The Magnificent Mile's retail vacancy rate increased from 15% in 2019 to 34% in 2023. Brookfield forfeited Water Tower Place to avoid a $300 million foreclosure in 2022.

articles.title: OHT Partners plans $48M multifamily project in Farmers Branch
articles.narrative: OHT Partners is planning a new Class A multifamily development, Lenox Alpha, in Farmers Branch, a suburb of Dallas. The project, estimated to cost $48 million, will feature 391 units across five stories and is slated to begin construction in September 2026. The developer acquired the five-acre site in 2022, which is strategically located near the Dallas North Tollway.

data_points.notable_features: Class A, five-story wrap development located half a mile west of the Dallas North Tollway.
data_points.project_notes: Site previously acquired in 2022 from Ginco Investments.

articles.market_intelligence: The Dallas-area multifamily market entered the year with high supply and relatively low occupancy, posting a 92.6 percent occupancy rate last year. Apartment construction activity has decreased in the Dallas area for eleven straight quarters.

articles.title: Austin’s multifamily and commercial fundamentals show growth
articles.narrative: Despite recent narratives suggesting a mass exodus, Austin's commercial real estate fundamentals show resilience and continued growth, according to market experts. The city's population surpassed 1 million in 2025, growing 4.2% since 2020, and it continues to attract corporate relocations to the Sun Belt. While the multifamily market experienced a significant oversupply with 107,000 units delivered in the past six years, leading to a peak vacancy rate of 16% in 2024, absorption rates have been historically high, and the development pipeline is now shrinking. This, combined with strong job growth, suggests a positive outlook for rent growth by 2027.
articles.market_intelligence: Austin's multifamily vacancy rate peaked at 16% in 2024, significantly higher than the national average of 9.3%. Rent growth is projected to turn positive in 2027. Austin's job growth between April 2025 and April 2026 was 1.2%, outpacing the statewide 0.8% and nationwide 0.2% figures. The city has added at least 4,000 residents each year in the first half of the decade.

articles.title: Endless supply: DFW residential rankings
articles.narrative: This article ranks the top residential broker teams and brokerages in the Dallas-Fort Worth market by total dollar volume, highlighting the continued strength of the region's housing market despite economic headwinds. Ben Caballero of HomesUSA.com led the individual broker rankings with $2.43 billion in sales, while Compass topped the brokerage list with over $6.2 billion. The report also delves into the unique dynamics of DFW's luxury market, where local firm Allie Beth Allman & Associates maintains a strong presence against national competitors like Compass, often leveraging off-market transactions.
articles.market_intelligence: Home construction in Texas has seen a decline from its post-pandemic peak. Interest rate reductions expected for the current year do not appear to be materializing, which is anticipated to lower housing starts and reduce inventory. Dallas-Fort Worth led the state in sales dollar volume for ultra-luxury homes in 2025, with 15 homes selling above $10 million, surpassing Houston's 10. Off-market transactions are common in Dallas, partly to avoid high property tax valuations, and these deals significantly boost top luxury teams.

articles.title: AT&T adds insult to injury with replica of Dallas’ Reunion Tower on Plano campus
articles.narrative: AT&T is developing a 54-acre campus in Plano, Texas, which will include a 280-foot replica of Dallas' Reunion Tower, serving as a communications antenna. This move follows AT&T's decision to relocate its headquarters from Downtown Dallas, a move that will leave a significant vacancy in the city's office market. The Plano campus, which received a $20 million incentive package from the city, is expected to be completed in the second half of 2028.

data_points.notable_features: Campus will feature a 280-foot replica of Dallas' Reunion Tower, doubling as a communication antenna.
data_points.project_notes: Plano provided a $20 million incentives package to attract AT&T. AT&T's lease at Whitacre Tower in Downtown Dallas runs until 2031.

articles.title: New generation of AI tenants rewrites culture of office leasing
articles.market: New York City, NY / San Francisco, CA
articles.narrative: The rapid expansion of artificial intelligence firms is significantly reshaping the office leasing landscape in New York City and San Francisco, creating a new class of tenants with distinct demands and accelerated timelines. These AI startups, often flush with venture capital, are driving a frenetic pace of leasing, with deals closing in weeks rather than months, and prioritizing speed and certainty over protracted negotiations. Landlords are adapting to these cash-rich but potentially volatile tenants by requesting substantial security deposits, while brokers are navigating unconventional requests and a compressed deal-making process. This shift is particularly evident in San Francisco, where AI companies accounted for nearly 40% of Q1 2026 deal volume, and in New York City, where they represented over a third of tech sector demand.

data_points.address: 275 Seventh Avenue, New York City, NY

people.name: Liz Hart
people.title: President of Leasing for North America

articles.title: In the AI boom, even San Francisco’s Class A buildings need renovations
articles.narrative: Lone Star Funds, which acquired the debt and subsequently the ownership of the 20-story, 360,000-square-foot office tower at 600 California Street in San Francisco, has partnered with Harvest Properties. Harvest Properties has purchased an equity stake in the building and plans significant renovations, including a full-floor amenity space spec buildout, to attract tenants in the burgeoning artificial intelligence sector. The building was only 25 percent occupied at the time of the partnership announcement, highlighting the need for upgrades to compete in the current market.

data_points.notable_features: Class A office tower in the Financial District, formerly owned by WeWork, planning major renovations including a full-floor amenity space spec buildout.
data_points.project_notes: Lone Star Funds acquired the debt backed by the tower in December for approximately $130 million, then became the owner after no offers were made at the foreclosure auction in April.

articles.market_intelligence: The AI leasing boom is driving demand for pre-furnished offices with extensive amenity spaces in San Francisco, prompting landlords of even trophy assets like Salesforce Tower to consider spec suite buildouts. Competitors are reportedly spending up to $40 million on full-floor renovations to attract new tenants. The AI frenzy has also tightened the San Francisco apartment market, with median one-bedroom rents crossing $4,000 per month and increasing 21% year-over-year, while national rents remained flat or slightly decreased. California Assembly Bill 1903, which aims to reform condo defect liability laws to encourage more condo development, has passed the Assembly and is moving to the Senate.

articles.title: Bellforge Arts Center Breaks Ground at Former Medfield Hospital Campus
articles.narrative: The Bellforge Arts Center has commenced a $30 million preservation and renovation project at the former Medfield State Hospital campus, aiming to transform the historic Lee Chapel and Infirmary into a year-round arts and education hub. The initiative is funded through a capital campaign and historic tax credits, with doors anticipated to open in fall 2027. This development is part of a broader redevelopment effort for the former hospital campus, supported by state and local infrastructure investments.

data_points.property_name: Bellforge Arts Center (Lee Chapel and Infirmary)
data_points.notable_features: Preservation and renovation of historic Lee Chapel and Infirmary into a year-round arts and education campus.
data_points.project_notes: Project is part of the larger redevelopment of the Medfield State Hospital campus.

articles.financing: Funded through Bellforge's $25 million capital campaign and historic tax credits through the Massachusetts Historic Commission.

articles.title: Crescent Debuts 151-Unit Lawrenceville BTR Community
articles.narrative: Crescent Communities has opened Harmon Cedar Run, a 151-unit build-to-rent community in Lawrenceville, GA. This project marks Crescent's 13th multifamily investment in the Atlanta market and its first under the Harmon brand. The community offers a mix of townhomes and single-family houses for rent, featuring two-car garages and a range of amenities including a pickleball court and EV charging.

data_points.notable_features: Includes a mix of townhomes and single-family houses, two-car garages, pickleball and basketball court, large pool, playground, pet spaces, and EV outlets in each garage.
data_points.project_notes: This is the first project under Crescent Communities' Harmon brand. The Harmon portfolio is supported by a joint venture partnership with an account managed by Heitman LLC, which recently increased its commitment from $235 million to $345 million.

articles.title: Converted Belmont Mill Trades for $55.5M
articles.narrative: Harbor Group International has acquired Chronicle Mill, a 238-unit adaptive-reuse apartment complex with 9,000 square feet of commercial space in Belmont, NC, from Armada Hoffler Realty Trust for $55.5 million. This transaction is part of a larger $562 million portfolio acquisition by Harbor Group, involving 11 multifamily properties from Armada Hoffler. The Chronicle Mill, originally built in 1901, underwent a $60 million redevelopment before this sale.

data_points.notable_features: Adaptive-reuse apartment complex with commercial space, offering studio, 1-bed, and 2-bed units.
data_points.project_notes: The 2.6-acre site was previously acquired by Armada Hoffler Realty Trust for $1.4 million in 2020. This property was the 28th textile mill built in Gaston County and the first in Belmont.

articles.title: Inland Waterfront Destination Slated for Miramar
articles.narrative: Sunbeam and Stiles have broken ground on Miramar Cove, a 125-acre mixed-use development in Miramar, FL, which they are positioning as South Florida's first inland waterfront destination. The project, with an estimated cost of up to $1 billion, will feature a significant water component, including a Blue Mar Basin with translucent blue water, surrounded by retail, office, residential, and hospitality components. The development aims to create a vibrant community hub with dining, entertainment, and gathering spaces centered around the waterfront.

data_points.notable_features: Nearly one-third of the site is dedicated to water, including a Blue Mar Basin with translucent blue water and fountain shows.
data_points.project_notes: The project includes 400,000 SF of retail (with a 35,000 SF grocer), 125,000 SF of Class A office, 185 hotel keys, and 2,874 residential units.
